title: Gentle Mexican Beef Stew
description: A rich and velvety Mexican stew featuring minced beef, perfect for a hangover cure, made in a terracotta dish with lacto-vegetarian friendly ingredients. This slow-cooked stew is a hearty and comforting Sonoran dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the minced beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Transfer the beef and onion mixture to a terracotta dish.
Add the diced tomatoes, beef broth, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the terracotta dish and stir to combine.
Cover the dish with a lid or foil and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the flavors have melded together and the meat is tender.
Remove the dish from the oven and stir in the shredded monterey jack cheese until it is melted and the stew is creamy.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or scallions if desired.
